experiment: MAGMA Level-2 ground-truth reachability — oracle verification step

oracle_integrity.note: reached and triggered are separate, both functional. The triggered counter demonstrably fires for other bugs, so PNG001_T=0 is a real 'not triggered', not a dead counter. Impossible to read 'no crash = not reached'.

key_finding: PNG001 triggered=0 is STRUCTURAL, not budget-limited. The MAGMA harness does not call png_set_user_limits, so the default user_width_max=1,000,000 rejects the width 0x55555555 that the overflow needs, at png_check_IHDR, before it can ever reach the row_factor divide with the bad value. No fuzzing budget changes this. This independently confirms, on MAGMA ground truth, the benchmarks/harness_reasoning result: the bug sits behind a limit the default harness does not raise.
implication_for_nemesis_arm: Raising the limit (Arm B = harness + png_set_user_limits) is NECESSARY to make PNG001 triggerable at all. But the trigger also needs a width=0x55555555 PNG with a VALID IHDR CRC (the CRC gate), which blind fuzzing rarely synthesises in a short budget. So Arm B may still show triggered=0 unless a seed supplies the width. The clean harness-edge on PNG001 is therefore the deterministic A/B already in benchmarks/harness_reasoning; MAGMA's contribution here is the canary-backed proof that the baseline is STRUCTURALLY blocked, plus a verified oracle for targets where fuzzing can reach the trigger.

fixed_conditions.note: These must be identical for the NEMESIS arm so the harness is the only variable.
